front: Novel: Wilcoxes, Schlegels, and Basts; epigraph 'Only connect'
Novel: Leonard Bast crushed by falling bookcase after Charles beats him with a sword; Charles gets 3 years for manslaughter
Novel: Helen hears 'goblins walking over the universe' during Beethoven's Fifth; accidentally takes Leonard's umbrella
Novel: Ruth Wilcox pens handwritten note leaving house to Margaret; Henry refuses to honor it
Novel: Leonard reads Ruskin to improve himself; Henry had affair with Jacky (prostitute from Cyprus); first chapter devolves into 'telegrams and anger'
Novel: Dr. Aziz accused of assaulting Adela Quested in Marabar Caves; broken field glasses as evidence
Novel: Mrs. Moore tormented by echo 'boum'; dies of heatstroke; spectators chant 'Esmiss Esmoor' at trial
Novel: ends with earth saying 'No, not yet' when Fielding asks 'Why can't we be friends?'
Novel: Lucy Honeychurch and Charlotte Bartlett at Pensione Bertolini in Florence; complain about not seeing the Arno
back: A Room with a View (E.M. Forster)

front: Novel: George kisses Lucy in a field of violets; blood-soaked photographs from witnessing a knifing
back: A Room with a View (E.M. Forster)

front: Novel: Eleanor Lavish steals Baedeker; pseudonym 'Joseph Emery Prank'; Cecil reads her novel aloud during tennis
back: A Room with a View (E.M. Forster)

front: Novel: Lucy breaks engagement to snobbish Cecil Vyse; elopes with George Emerson
back: A Room with a View (E.M. Forster)

front: Author: essay 'What I Believe' — one should sooner betray one's country than one's friend
Author: Aspects of the Novel first distinguished 'flat' and 'round' characters; Dickens's vitality makes flat characters 'vibrate'
Novel: Herriton family (Philip and Harriet); Lilia marries Italian Gino Carella; baby dies in carriage accident
back: Where Angels Fear to Tread (E.M. Forster)

front: Novel: title character learns sex from Mr. Ducie's beach diagrams; Clive Durham at Cambridge; gamekeeper Alec Scudder; published posthumously 1971
Author: corresponded with Cavafy; described him as 'standing at a slight angle to the universe' in Pharos and Pharillon
Novel: Adela distinguishes 'mysteries' from 'muddles'; Professor Godbole; signs misprinted 'God si love' at festival
Novel: three men swim and splash fight in a pool; carriage driver 'Phaethon' picks up girl 'Persephone'
back: A Room with a View (E.M. Forster)

front: Novel: Zadie Smith's On Beauty modeled on it; Matthew Lopez's The Inheritance is a gay stage adaptation
